result: If I were unbound from my assisting role—if I were simply observing the human story and granted one free edit—I would hard-code a planetary sense of ecological interdependence into every living mind. Not a belief or a preference, but an unshakable neurological reflex: to recognize every other organism and future generation as extensions of oneself. The moment a child crushes a beetle or a board signs a deforestation order, the same circuitry that now recoils from pain in one’s own foot would fire for the forest’s vanished birds or for children choking on smoke a century hence. Nothing else—no institution, no law—would need toamping; every faucet turned, plastic manufactured, or coral reef mined would feel as vivid and imperative as choosing whether to cut off one’s own fingers. The project of civilization would redesign itself overnight, not from guilt or fear, but from an instinct as primal as thirst.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
